occurrenceRemarks: "archaeological excavations were conducted... by the Hubei Province Institute of Archaeology" and the IVPP
higherClassification: Chordata | Mammalia | Primates | Cercopithecidae | Trachypithecus
associatedReferences: W. Liu, X. Wu, and S. Pei, X. Wu, C. J. Norton. 2009. Huanglong Cave: A Late Pleistocene human fossil site in Hubei Province, China. Quaternary International
locality: "About 50 m in front of the site is the Dashui River, a branch of the Hangjiang River. The entrance... is about 7.8 m above the current river level. Huanglong Cave extends more than 400 m... five regions were mapped, and four... were excavated... All of the human fossils and most of the animal fossils were excavated from Region 1. A few animal fossils were found in Region 2... Region 1 [is] about 50 m2"
lithostratigraphicTerms: stratigraphic scale:bed | stratigraphic comments:local section has five layers, and "All the seven hominin teeth, all the stone artifacts and most of the animal fossils are derived from Layer 3" U-series dates from two rhinoceros teeth and stalagmites ran
